title: Rennell and Bellona Business Licence Ordinance 2014 (LN 15, 2015)
text:
107
SUPPLEMENT
to the Solomon Islands Gazette
Wednesday 4th March, 2015 S.1. No.12
[Legal Notice No.IS)
RENNELL AND BELLONA PROVINCE BUSINESS LICENCE
ORDINANCE 2014
1. Title and commencement
(1) This Ordinance may be cited as the Rennell and Bellona
Province Business Licence Ordinance 2014.
(2) This Ordinance:
(a) in enacted by the Rennell and Bellona Provincial
Assembly under section 31 of the Provincial
Government Act 1997; and
(b) comes into force on the day that it is published in the
Solomon Islands Gazette.
2. Interpretation
In this Ordinance, unless context otherwise requires:
Act means the Provincial Government Act 1997.
Assembly means the Rennell and Bellona Provincial Assembly
constituted under section 7 of the Act.
Authorised Officer means any officer authorised by the Executive in
accordance with section 19 of this Ordinance.
Business means any activity carried out in order to make a profit,
including:
(a) provision of services;
(b) manufacturing;
(c) retailing;
(d) wholesaling;
(e) harvesting or collection of natural resources;
(f) farming;
(g) purchasing goods within the Province for sale outside
the Province;
(h) working on contract for another business;
but does not include being an employee or activities on or in a
local market, or hawking.

108
Executive means the Rennell and Bellona Provincial Executive
established under section 19 of the Act.
Person means any person, persons, company, or other group,
association or co-operative but does not include any government
department or the Assembly.
Premises means any structure, building, the land on which it is situated
and any adjoining land used in connection with the structure or
building.
Province means Rennell and Bellona Province as defined under
section 3 of the Act.
Provincial means relating to Rennell and Bellona Province.
Religious material means items directly related to the carrying out of
religious services, such as wine, candles, dictionaries, and bibles.
3. Purpose
The purpose of this Ordinance is to regulate and control the operation
of businesses in Rennell and Bellona province for the purpose of
protecting and promoting the people, culture, and environment of
Rennell and Bellona Province.
4. Business must be licensed
No person may operate any business in the Province unless they hold
a current business licence.
5. Exemptions
(1) This Ordinance does not apply to:
(a) the Rennell and Bellona Development Authority;
(b) hawking or trading in a local market or local garden
produce, prepared custom foods, custom handicrafts
and similar local produce;
(c) fundraising where the proceeds are used solely for
charitable, religious, educational, sporting or other
community purposes; or
(d) selling of religious books and other religious material
or paraphernalia.
6. Form of application
(1) Subject to section 7, ana application for a first time business
licence must be:
(a) in the form prescribed in Schedule 1;
(b) sent to the Provincial Secretary; and
(c) accompanied by the fee prescribed in Schedule 5.
(2) An application for renewal of a business licence must be:
(a) in the form prescribed in Schedule 2;
(b) sent to the Provincial Secretary; and
(c) accompanied by the fee prescribed in Schedule 5.

]09
(3) Subject to section 18 each type of business operating within or on
premises must be separately licensed.
(4) No person may apply for a business licence if:
(a) they have outstanding business licence fees owing to the
province;
(b) they have not obtained the required national licences,
permits, or permissions;
(c) in the case of foreign investment, they are carrying out
business in a reserved or prohibited area; or
(d) they are conducting business in contravention of any
National or Provincial law or policy.
7. Applications for mining and logging operations
(1) Applications relating to mining and logging operations must be
accompanied by copies of the following:
(a) the business's national licence issued by the Director of
Mines and/or the Commissioner of Forest Resources;
(b) Approval from the Director of the Environment and
Conservation Division to carry out the activities described
in the business licence application and to which the
national licence relate;
(c) a copy of the company's Environmental Impact
Assessment; and
(d) any other documentation and information that the
Provincial Executive may require in order to assess the
potential impact of the activities for which a licence is
sought.
(2) No licence shall be issued for mining and logging operations until
and unless the information in subsection(l) is provided.
8. Decisions about applications
(1) All applications shall be referred to the Provincial Executive who
shall:
(a) approve the application;
(b) decline the application; or
(c) approve the application subject to any terms and
conditions imposed under section 19.
(2) When considering any application under subsection (1), the
Executive may take into account:
(a) the extent of involvement in the business by the people
indigenous toi Rennell and Bellona Province and resident
in the locality where the business is intended to be carried
out;
(b) the contribution to services to the people of Rennell and
Bellona Province;
(c) the geographical area served by the business; or
(d) any other matter or matters affecting the interests of the
people of Rennell and Bellona and their environment.
J

110
(3) If the Executiveis considering declining an application under
subsection J(b), it must first give the applicant:
(a) notice of the reasons that the application might be declined;
and
(b) a reasonable opportunity to make a submission or be heard
on the matter.
9. Executive may impose terms and conditions
(1) The Executive may impose any terms and conditions on a business
licence that it considers reasonable in the circumstances.
(2) If the Executive is considering imposing a term and condition under
subsection (1), it must first give the applicant:
(i) notice of the reasons why the terms and condition might be
imposed; and
(ii) a reasonable opportunity to make a submission or be heard
on the matter.
(3) The Executive may by written notice remove, amend, revoke, or
add to any term and condition imposed under subsection (1).
10. Person deemed to hold a licence from receipt of application
An applicant is deemed to hold a licence from the date that their application
iss received by the Provincial Secretary or Authorised Officer.
11. Duration of licence
A licence has effect from the date that it is issued until 31 March following
date of issue.
12. Classification of business
(1) The Provincial Secretary shall in the first instance determine the
classification of business to which the application relates.
(2) If the applicant disputes the determination made by the Provincial
Secretary under subsection (1), the applicant may apply to the
Executive for reconsideration of the determination and may make
written or oral submissions in support of their application.
13. Form of licence
(1) Upon approval by the Executive or a person authorised under
section 20, the applicant shall be issued a business licence in the
form prescribed in Schedule 3.
(2) The production of this document or a certified copy issued under
section 17, shall be proof that the licence has been properly granted.
J

111
14. Register of business licences
(1) The Provincial Treasurer shall keep a register of all:
(a) applications for a business licence;
(b) decisions made in respect of each application; and
(c) licences issued under this Ordinance.
(2) The register must be open for public inspection during normal
working hours at the place where it is kept.
15. Display of licence
(1) A business licence must be:
(a) displayed in a prominent position in or on the business
premises; and
(b) available for public inspection during normal working
hours.
16. Transfer of licence
A business licence is personal to the applicant and not transferable.
17. Lost, stolen or damaged licences
A licence holder whose licence has been lost, stolen or damaged may,
on payment of the prescribed fee, obtain a certified copy of the licence
from the Provincial Treasurer.
18. Suspension, cancellation, or revocation of licence
(1) The Executive may suspend, cancel or revoke a business
licence if it has reason to believe that the business:
(a) has not compiled with a term and condition of their
licence; or
(b) is carrying out their business in a manner that brings
the province into disrepute.
(2) If the Executive is considering suspending, cancelling or
revoking a licence under subsection (1), it must first give the
applicant:
(a) notice of the reasons why the suspension, cancellation,
or revocation is being considered; and
(b) a reasonable opportunity to make a submission and be
heard on the matter.
19. Application for additional premises
(1) Any licence holder may apply to the Provincial Executive for
permission to operate their business in additional premises.

112
(2) Any application under subsection (1) must:
(a) be addressed to the Provincial Secretary;
(b) be in the form prescribed in Schedule 4;
(c) be accompanied by the prescribed fee.
(3) If a fee is not prescribed, the Provincial Executive may fix
a fee, being a fee reasonable in the circumstances.
20. Executive may authorise persons
(1) The Executive may authorise a person ("Authorised
Officer") to perform functions, duties, and exercise powers
under and in accordance with the Ordinance.
(2) The Executive may impose any restrictions, limitations, or
requirements on an authorisation issued under subsection (1).
(3) The Executive may suspend, cancel or revoke an
authorisation issued under subsection (1) at any time.
(4) Any authorisation issued under subsection (1), or suspension,
cancellation or revocation issued under subsection (3), must
be:
(a) in writing; and
(b) served on the Authorised Officer
21. Business licence fee must be paid into Provincial Fund
(1) Any fee due under this Ordinance must be paid into the
Provincial Fund of Rennell and Bellona Province, as
established under section 34 of the Act.
(2) Any business that does not deposit their licence fee into
the Provincial Fund is liable for a fine of two times the
amount of the fee due under subsection (1).
22. All mining licensee to pay a bond of $1 ,500,000 into a trust account
as security to provincial government.
23. All logging licensee to pay a bond of $500,000. Into a trust account
as security to provincial government treasury Division.
24. Enforcement of fees, fines and costs
Every fee imposed under this Ordinance, fines incurred under
sections 2] (2) and 23, or costs incurred in the enforcement of this
Ordinance, shall be recoverable in any court of competent
jurisdiction as a debt due to the Province.

miFt1"wloIIIj., mczztT'p'w'1'7tSe'rrtwt1wtriifW*.W
113
25. Offences
(I) Any person who operates a business without a current business
licence commits an offence and shall be liable to a fine not
exceeding:
(a) $500.00 in the case of a first time offence; or
(b) $2,000 in the case of a second, or repeat, offence.
For Foreigners:
I. mining
a. licensee- not more than $50,000. And not less than
$20,000
b. contractor- not more than $50,000. And not less
than $20,000
11. logging
a. licensee- not more than $50,000. And not less than
$20,000
b. contractor- not more than $50,000. And not less
than $20,000
iii. fisheries- not more than $50,000. And not less than $20,000
IV. shipping- not more than $50,000. And not less than $20,000
(2) Any person operating a business after receiving notification that their
business licence has been suspended, cancelled or revoked shall be
guilty of an offence and liable to a fine not exceeding $200.00.
(3) Any person who continues to operate a business having been found
guilty of an offence under either subsection 0) or (3), commits an
offence and shall be liable to a fine not exceeding $2,000.00.
(4) Any person who obstructs a Police Officer or Authorised Officer,
commits an offence and shall liable to a fine not exceeding $200.00.
26. Transitional provisions
Any business licence issued under a previous shall have continuing effect
up to but not exceeding the first day of April immediately following the
introduction of this Ordinance.
27. Revocation
This Ordinance revokes all previous Ordinances relating to licensing of
business
28. Review
This ordinance subject to a review after one year.

121
SCHEDULE
PART II
CALCULATION OF FEES
1. In the case of the first licence issuance, the Licence Holder shall pay
the fee for his business classification as calculated for the balance of
that licence year by the Provincial Treasurer.
(a) 1st quarter April, May, June Full year fee
(b) 2nd quarter July, August, September 3/4 year fee
(c) 3rd quarter October, November,
December Half year fee
(d) 4th quarter January, February, March Quarter year fee
NB. All applications must be submitted with a non-refundable fee
of $20.00
2. Any licence holder whose licence expires of December 31 may renew
their licence by application to the Provincial Treasurer and payment of
the appropriate fee for the 4th quarter January through to March. The
licence holder may also renew for the full licence year commencing
April, each year by payment of the full year licence fee for their
business classification on or before April 30.

122
SCHEDULE I
PART III
ANCILLARY FEES

Endorsement of transfer of licence $100.00
